content:
Title: Printer Troubleshooting Guide

If a user reports a printer not working or showing an error code:
1. Ask for the exact error code displayed on the printer screen, if any.
2. Check for paper jams by opening all printer trays and covers.
3. Power cycle the printer: turn it off, wait 10 seconds, turn it back on.
4. Check that the printer has paper and toner/ink.
5. Confirm the printer is connected to the network (check WiFi or ethernet connection on the printer's display).
6. If error persists after these steps, escalate to hardware support for possible part replacement.

Title: Granting Access to Shared Drives or Folders

If a user requests access to a shared drive, folder, or team resource:
1. Confirm the user's full name, employee ID, and which team or department they belong to.
2. Verify with the user's manager or team lead that they should have this access.
3. Do not grant access without manager confirmation for sensitive or restricted folders.
4. Once approved, grant the minimum level of access needed (read-only vs edit, if applicable).
5. Document the access change with date, approver name, and reason.

Title: Password Reset Procedure

If a user is locked out of their account or forgot their password:
1. Verify the user's identity by confirming their full name and employee ID.
2. Do not reset a password without identity verification, even if the request seems urgent.
3. Send a password reset link to the user's registered recovery email or phone number.
4. Advise the user to choose a password with at least 12 characters, including numbers and symbols.
5. If the user cannot verify their identity through standard means, escalate to the security team.

Title: Handling Software Crashes (e.g. Excel, Word, Browser)

If a user reports a program crashing or freezing:
1. Ask which specific program is crashing and what they were doing when it happened.
2. Ask the user to close and reopen the program.
3. Check if the program needs an update; recommend updating to the latest version.
4. If using Microsoft Office, suggest opening the program in Safe Mode to rule out faulty add-ins.
5. Check available disk space on the user's device; low disk space can cause crashes.
6. If the issue is isolated to one large file, suggest checking if the file itself is corrupted.
7. Escalate to software support if crashes continue after these steps.

Title: Troubleshooting WiFi Connection Issues

If a user reports WiFi disconnecting or not connecting:
1. Ask the user to restart their device and the WiFi router/modem.
2. Check if other devices on the same network are also affected. If yes, this is likely a router or ISP issue, not the device.
3. Ask the user to forget the WiFi network on their device and reconnect, re-entering the password.
4. Check if the device is too far from the router, causing signal weakness.
5. If the issue persists after these steps, escalate to network admin to check router logs and firmware version.
